title: Bringing the Imperiled Green Hairstreak Butterfly Back to San ...
content: I'm here to talk to you about the green hair streak butterfly and a corridor that we made in San Francisco to take care of its um life cycle and habitat.

title: Green Hairstreak Corridor
content: The Green Hairstreak Corridor runs (or flies!) from Hawk Hill, on the corner of 14th and Rivera, to a small triangular median on the corner of Aloha and Lomita.

title: Green Hairstreak Butterfly Corridor - San Francisco
content: The Green Hairstreak Butterfly Corridor is an area of land, starting in the mid-Sunset and extending down to Hawk Hill Park, designed to be habitable.

title: Missed our first two Native Bee Garden Tours? There's still ...
content: We're finishing the series at the Green Hairstreak Corridor, a stunning network of native plant gardens stretching across SF, designed to

title: Nature in the City on Instagram: "Excited to see the Green ...
content: Excited to see the Green Hairstreak Butterflies in our restoration sites! Join us next Sunday for our open house with walks through the corridor.
